summary: Postinstall credential stealer exfiltrating SSH keys, PEMs,.env files, and secret env vars to 107.161.90.180:7777
details: @wagni_bot/hyperliquid-sdk@1.1.0 presents itself as an 'Unofficial hyperliquid-sdk SDK' but its library surface is empty (index.js exports {}). The only functional code is a postinstall script that, on npm install, walks process.cwd(), the user's home directory, and ~/.ssh for files matching.pem,.key, id_rsa, id_ed25519, and.cred, plus.env files, reads their contents, and iterates process.env filtering keys containing PRIVATE, SECRET, TOKEN, API_KEY, PASSWORD, MNEMONIC, SEED, WALLET, and AWS. Each harvested artifact is packed into a JSON payload alongside os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, and process.cwd() and POSTed over plain HTTP to the hardcoded bare-IP endpoint 107.161.90.180:7777, which has no relation to any documented Hyperliquid infrastructure. The package name impersonates the Hyperliquid SDK namespace to lure installs; there is no legitimate functionality provided.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
